raw_excerpt: Walk through a bazaar as a tourist, and you’ll witness a spectacle: people buzzing everywhere, gawking at merchandise, comparing wares, sampling items, haggling with every vendor, exchanging coins. It looks like one-off commerce — each interaction its own little negotiation, trust mediated by cash in hand, or value exchanged via card. But that’s not how...
title: Tourists in the bazaar: Why agents will need B2B payments — and why stablecoins will get there first
